element_introduction_order.first_appears: Rolling doubles three times or landing on Go to Jail

typical_relief_triggers: Landing on unowned property with insufficient funds to purchase
Getting out of Jail in late game
Successful property trade reducing debt pressure
typical_tension_triggers: Drawing Community Chest or Chance cards with negative outcomes
Being sent to Jail with active properties generating rent
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based with escalating financial pressure; early game exploratory, mid-game competitive acquisition, late-game survival-focused. Pacing controlled by die rolls and player decisions rather than time.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low pressure - abundant cash, property acquisition encourages activity, limited financial consequence
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate escalation - property monopolies forming, rent payment begins straining cash reserves, trading decisions become critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: High pressure - developed properties generate severe rent, Jail becomes strategic shelter rather than penalty, AI exploitability increases vulnerability to bankruptcy
notable_spikes: First major rent payment from developed property
Transition from property acquisition to house/hotel building phase
Late-game multiple consecutive rent payments forcing liquidation

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression from economic expansion (purchasing properties) to consolidation (building improvements) to contraction (bankruptcy elimination). No level-based structure; game is one continuous economy simulation.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Circular spatial loop with strategic positions
notable_level_patterns.description: Board is continuous loop (Go → properties 1-40 → back to Go). Key tension nodes at railroads, utilities, and Free Parking. Go to Jail acts as circuit-breaker mechanic.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Board layout - spaces 5 (railroad), 20 (Free Parking), 30 (Go to Jail)

notable_level_patterns.description: German version exposes AI flaw where computer players undervalue properties in trades (prices not doubled when currency was). Creates exploitable advantage phase in mid-game.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Trading phase any point after AI acquires property set
